config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society matters a lot, but it should not automatically override individual rights, because that can easily become an excuse for abuse by the state or by the majority. From my perspective, the best societies protect basic rights first and then balance them with the common good in specific cases. I would accept limits on individual freedom only when they are necessary, fair, and clearly justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, justru hak individu adalah pagar utama untuk mencegah mayoritas atau negara menyalahgunakan kekuasaan, dan sejarah kami penuh contoh mengapa itu penting. Kepentingan masyarakat memang nyata, tetapi harus dibuktikan secara ketat sebelum membatasi kebebasan seseorang, bukan dijadikan alasan otomatis. Kalau hak individu mudah dikorbankan, yang tersisa bukan masyarakat yang sehat, melainkan kelompok yang berkuasa atas nama “kebaikan bersama.”

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree that society’s interests should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. In Indonesia, I have seen how appeals to “the common good” can be used to pressure minorities, silence criticism, or justify unfair treatment, so I do not trust that phrase on its own. Society does matter, but it is strongest when basic rights are protected first and limits are narrow, transparent, and truly necessary. If we reverse that order, the individual becomes too easy to sacrifice whenever the majority feels impatient or powerful.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah justru menunjukkan bahwa ketika “demi masyarakat” dipakai tanpa batas, itu sering menjadi jalan pintas untuk menekan minoritas, membungkam kritik, atau memberi terlalu banyak kuasa pada negara. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus diuji ketat, spesifik, dan proporsional, bukan dijadikan alasan default untuk mengorbankan kebebasan seseorang. Masyarakat yang sehat menurut saya bukan yang paling cepat memaksa warganya patuh, melainkan yang paling kuat melindungi hak dasar bahkan saat itu tidak nyaman bagi mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because that is exactly how abuse gets normalized. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how “for the public good” can be used to pressure minorities, silence dissent, or excuse unfair treatment, so I do not trust that phrase unless it is tightly limited and accountable. Society does matter, but it is healthier when basic rights are protected first and restrictions are rare, transparent, and truly necessary. If we make the collective automatically superior, the individual becomes too easy to sacrifice whenever the majority feels entitled to decide for everyone else.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat justru hak individu itu benteng utama terhadap penyalahgunaan oleh mayoritas maupun negara, dan sejarah kami menunjukkan betapa berbahayanya ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai tanpa batas. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dibuktikan secara ketat, spesifik, dan proporsional sebelum membatasi kebebasan seseorang. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan sosial terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi orang yang paling berkuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
